name: opensearch
title: OpenSearch DevTools
description: Query and manage OpenSearch clusters — API explorer, command presets, and search, keyboard-first.
author: itggood2420

commands.name: console
commands.title: Console
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Run requests in OpenSearch Dashboards Dev Tools console syntax

commands.name: api-explorer
commands.title: API Explorer
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Send an arbitrary request to OpenSearch and inspect the JSON response

commands.name: command-palette
commands.title: Command Palette
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Run common OpenSearch commands such as Cluster Health or Cat Indices

commands.name: search
commands.title: Search
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Pick an index and run a search query

commands.name: query-history
commands.title: Query History
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Browse, re-run, and favorite recent queries

commands.name: manage-connections
commands.title: Manage Connections
commands.subtitle: OpenSearch
commands.description: Add and edit OpenSearch cluster connections and pick the default one

tools.name: run-query
tools.title: Run OpenSearch Query
tools.description: Send a REST request (search, cat, cluster, mapping, etc.) to the active OpenSearch connection and return the JSON response

ai.instructions:
You can query the user's OpenSearch cluster with the run-query tool. Requests target the active connection (the default saved connection, or the preferences fallback). Rules:
- Paths must start with '/'.
- List indices with `GET /_cat/indices?format=json`.
- Read a field mapping with `GET /{index}/_mapping`.
- Search with `GET /{index}/_search` (or POST) and a JSON body containing a `query`. Default `size` to 10 unless the user asks otherwise.
- Cluster status: `GET /_cluster/health`. Node/cluster info: `GET /_nodes`, `GET /_cluster/stats`.
- Only use PUT or DELETE when the user clearly asks to modify or delete data; these require confirmation.
- Summarize responses concisely. When returning documents, highlight the key fields instead of dumping the entire raw JSON.

preferences.name: defaultUrl
preferences.title: OpenSearch URL
preferences.description: Fallback connection used when no connection has been saved yet

preferences.name: username
preferences.title: Username
preferences.description: Username for the fallback connection (Basic Auth)

preferences.name: password
preferences.title: Password
preferences.description: Password for the fallback connection (Basic Auth)

preferences.name: ignoreCerts
preferences.title: TLS
preferences.label: Ignore Certificate Errors (not recommended)
preferences.description: Skip TLS verification for self-signed certificates

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
